name: Decay-Core Civic Poster
rationale: A bold vintage WPA public-works propaganda poster being eaten alive by creeping orange algae-rot makes the institutional-corruption theme instantly legible and unsettling; clean civic optimism above, orange decay below is exactly the striking, shareable contrast that stops a scroll.
preamble: Vintage 1930s WPA / public-works propaganda-poster illustration: bold flat geometric shapes, heroic civic iconography, a limited screen-printed palette of cream, teal, and ochre with strong black outlines and stippled shading, monumental low-angle composition. The poster is CORRUPTED by a creeping orange algae-bloom rot that spreads across it like mold eating the ink, blistering and discoloring the lower portion. The contrast of clean civic optimism above and orange decay below carries the whole image. 4:5 portrait.
